title: viscum album subsp. meridianum
intro: Viscum album subsp. meridianum, also known as European mistletoe, is a type of semi-parasitic plant that grows on the branches of various tree species, obtaining its nutrients from the host plant while producing its own food through photosynthesis. The medicinal properties of European mistletoe include its ability to stimulate the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antiviral and anticancer activities. The health benefits of European mistletoe include its potential to reduce the severity and duration of colds and flu, alleviate symptoms of cancer and autoimmune diseases, and improve overall immune function. European mistletoe is typically prepared as a herbal extract or tincture, which can be taken orally or applied topically to promote its medicinal properties.
